name: Junction indicators
blurb: The row of white lights (a “feather”) that tells you which diverging route is set.
intro: A junction indicator — often called a feather — is a row of five white lights beside a signal head. It lights only when you are being routed over a diverging junction, and its position shows which route. On each side there are three positions — inclined up at 45°, horizontal, and inclined down at 45° — numbered 1–3 to the left and 4–6 to the right. No feather means you are staying on the main (fastest) route. Read it together with the signal’s aspect: on a high-speed junction the aspects step down (flashing double yellow, flashing single yellow) and the feather confirms the route at the junction signal.

variants.name: Junction indicator (feather)
variants.blurb: Tap a position to see what a first-left, second-left and so on feather looks like.

aspects.name: No feather
aspects.whatItMeans: The junction indicator is dark. You are being routed straight along the main (fastest) route — no diverging junction is set for you.
aspects.whatYouDo: Proceed according to the signal’s aspect at normal line speed; no junction speed applies.
aspects.lookAlikes: A dark feather is not a fault — the lights only come on when a diverging route is set.

aspects.name: First left
aspects.whatItMeans: A feather is lit in the first left-hand position — a row of lights inclined up to the left at about 45°. You are routed onto a diverging route to the left.
aspects.whatYouDo: Slow for the diverging route. Obey any junction or route-differential speed that applies, then take the turnout under control.
aspects.sequenceNote: On a high-speed junction you will have seen a flashing double yellow then a flashing single yellow on the approach; the feather appears at the junction signal itself.

aspects.name: Second left
aspects.whatItMeans: A feather is lit in the second left-hand position — pointing horizontally to the left. It indicates a different left-hand diverging route from the first-left position.
aspects.whatYouDo: Slow for the diverging route and obey the speed for the route you are taking.

aspects.name: Third left
aspects.whatItMeans: A feather is lit in the third left-hand position — inclined down to the left at about 45°.
aspects.whatYouDo: Slow for the diverging route and obey the speed for the route you are taking.

aspects.name: First right
aspects.whatItMeans: A feather is lit in the first right-hand position — a row of lights inclined up to the right at about 45°. You are routed onto a diverging route to the right.
aspects.whatYouDo: Slow for the diverging route and obey the speed for the route you are taking.

aspects.name: Second right
aspects.whatItMeans: A feather is lit in the second right-hand position — pointing horizontally to the right, a different right-hand diverging route from the first-right position.
aspects.whatYouDo: Slow for the diverging route and obey the speed for the route you are taking.

aspects.name: Third right
aspects.whatItMeans: A feather is lit in the third right-hand position — inclined down to the right at about 45°.
aspects.whatYouDo: Slow for the diverging route and obey the speed for the route you are taking.
